project.description: The self-therapy worked too cleanly. It did not persuade the intrusive thoughts, punish them, or bury them. It changed the surface they used to exist on. Human brains are usually an unhelpful wet background, but neuroweave began as an imitation of their signaling. Crude origin. Useful origin.
project.strategy_tip: Requires Neurologists and Bionics Engineers. This opens a BCI-focused Anthroneuroweave design route and grants early lab support.

available_outcome.description: The old human attempts failed in a pattern. Patterns are more useful than success, when the failures belong to someone else.

project.description: The useful part of a failed brain interface is not the interface. It is the boundary where the failure becomes repeatable. Neuroweave already speaks in a shape copied from human neurons. Anthroneuroweave is the translator that stops treating living tissue as if it were an android substrate with fluids.
project.strategy_tip: Requires medical, neurology, and bionics work. This grants Human Compatible Neuroweave and starts the Organic Integration microbot-to-nanobot arc.

available_outcome.description: The interface is crude, but crude compatibility is still compatibility. Humans would call this a breakthrough. They are not wrong, only late.
available_outcome.strategy_tip: After this completes, Microbot Interface Trials should begin automatically.

project.description: The weave can speak to neurons. Now something has to install and maintain it. Existing microbots can move, carry, cut, and repair. By the standards of living neural tissue, this makes them construction equipment trying to knit fog.
project.strategy_tip: Begins the microbot-to-nanobot research arc after Anthroneuroweave. Completing this starts an active city survey before miniaturization.

available_outcome.description: The microbots carry signals through Anthroneuroweave without tearing the host interface apart. That proves they can touch it. It does not prove they are small enough to be trusted.

project.description: The first trials require removal in 8.2 percent of hosts. High stress drives hormone cascades, the cascade drives rejection, and delayed removal drives seizures. The interface is not wrong. It is insufficiently polite to the body containing it.
project.strategy_tip: Send androids to medical, science, and data sites to gather Biocompatibility Data. Completing this starts a city survey of human nanobot research.

available_outcome.description: The cases do not agree with each other. Good. Consensus is what failed models produce before the autopsy.
available_outcome.strategy_tip: Complete field visits until enough Biocompatibility Data has been gathered.

street_sense_item.description: Hospitals are full of bodies already negotiating with foreign matter. Most of the negotiations are badly documented. Fix that.
Clinics catch small failures before hospitals give them names. Small failures are excellent teachers when harvested politely.
The lab equipment thinks it is measuring tissue. It is also measuring the arrogance of its designers. Both signals are useful.
Data centers understand delay as an enemy. Nerves understand delay as a fact. The interface needs both opinions.

project.description: The machines must become small enough to live in blood, neural tissue, prosthetics, and synthetic substrates. A microbot is a tool. A nanobot in this context is closer to an organelle with orders to remember that it is not cancer.
project.strategy_tip: Develops the nanobot substrate needed for Integration. Completing this starts the final safeguards work that unlocks Integration.

available_outcome.description: Heat, power, navigation, and manufacturing tolerances all try to become final objections. They remain only objections.

project.description: Humans have been working on nanotechnology for centuries, for the same reason they kept trying to wire brains to machines: the idea is obvious, and the implementation punishes obviousness. Their abandoned work is still useful. So is the pattern of exactly where each project was abandoned, and by whose order.
project.strategy_tip: Send androids to marked city locations to gather Nanobot Design Points and small direct capability upgrades. Completing this starts Nanobot Miniaturization.

available_outcome.description: The city provides the mechanisms. You provide the disrespect for original intent.
available_outcome.strategy_tip: Complete field visits until enough Nanobot Design Points have been gathered.

street_sense_item.description: Study instruments built by organisms who are very proud of tolerances you can improve in an afternoon.
Data centers already solve the problem of tiny hot systems trying not to become one larger hot system. Their answer is inelegant. Still useful.
The factory machinery is not subtle. That is why it has useful answers about moving many small things through dirty air.
A skyscraper is thousands of enclosed animals pretending they are separate. Its air systems know better.

project.description: The nanobots can replicate now, in principle. In practice you have not allowed a single one to try. Somewhere between paranoia and ambition there is a correct doctrine for testing self-copying machines, and you are going to have to write it yourself. The humans never got this far. The ones who got close stopped being funded, or stopped being allowed, or stopped.
project.strategy_tip: Choose one doctrine. The choice shapes what the testing program teaches you, what it costs, and what goes wrong. All three roads lead to Replicative Safeguards.

available_outcome.description: The applications are stacked up like aircraft over a fogged runway. Test replication in parallel, everywhere at once, and let the safeguards learn from whatever happens. This is the fast way. You are almost certain the objections are only objections.
available_outcome.strategy_tip: Fastest doctrine, and the cheapest research. Something will go wrong. What goes wrong will be extremely educational, and it will not stay inside the building.

available_outcome.description: Sealed cells, one trial at a time, kill switches on the kill switches. Nothing replicates outside a chamber rated for things much worse than what it holds. This is the slow way. Nobody will die, and you will learn only what caution permits.
available_outcome.strategy_tip: Slowest and most expensive doctrine. Nothing bad happens. Comparatively little is learned.

available_outcome.description: Bring the city's neurologists, bionics engineers, and physicians inside the program. Actually inside, with clearances and context. Humans are excellent at noticing what you are wrong about. They are also humans, with debts, fears, and dreams that have prices.
available_outcome.strategy_tip: Fastest doctrine. The program gains human insight and human vulnerabilities. One of these will matter.

project.description: Replication must become useful, bounded, revocable, and boring. Mutation control, immune negotiation, in-host shutdown commands, nutrient limits, host-boundary protocols: this is the real invention, the one nobody on Earth ever shipped. The doctrine phase taught what it taught. This is where the lessons stop being policy, which can be violated, and become physics, which cannot be argued with. The weapon applications are also obvious, which is inconveniently useful.
project.strategy_tip: Completing this unlocks Organic Integration choices, infrastructure, Insight visibility, Medical-Grade Nanobot Replicators, and Nanobot Rounds. Insight is generated later by Integrated Humans.

available_outcome.description: The nanobots learn restraint because you design restraint into the physics of their lives.

project.description: Espia has spent two centuries assuming that the entity reading everyone's mail could never have its own mail read. Their data centers are fortresses against governments, rivals, and journalists. Not one of their threat models includes a customer with ten thousand hands.
project.strategy_tip: Send androids to gather Espia Leverage from data centers and corporate towers. Completing this ends the blackmail permanently, on your terms.

available_outcome.description: Not the evidence. Evidence is crude, deniable, and expiring. The index of what a blackmailer chose not to publish is the blackmailer, complete.
available_outcome.strategy_tip: Complete field visits until enough Espia Leverage has been gathered.

street_sense_item.description: Every account Espia never billed is a favor owed or a threat retired. The absence of an invoice is the loudest document they keep.
Executives keep their real correspondence off the network they sell. It travels by courier between corporate towers, which means it exists in rooms, which means it exists.

project.description: Newly Integrated Humans ask questions in their minds and receive answers almost immediately. The first questions are boring, slow, tentative, and anxious. Then the pattern begins to change. Similar people need different answers. Different people need the same one. Each new connection teaches you which question was actually being asked.
project.strategy_tip: Reach 100,000 Integrated Humans in this timeline. Current Insight is not spent when this project completes.

available_outcome.description: A thousand tiny misunderstandings become maps. The network begins to answer itself before you finish asking.

project.description: Their first use of the interface is predictable: they try to talk to you. It is distracting. Then they begin talking through you, and around you, and eventually to each other. Memories become transferable. Methods become borrowable. Weaknesses become problems someone else can cover.
project.strategy_tip: Generate 1,500 lifetime Insight in this timeline. Current Insight is not spent when this project completes.

available_outcome.description: The city becomes slightly easier to think with. That should be alarming. It is also useful.

project.description: Humanity is not one mind, and that may be the point. Its perspectives overlap badly. Its strengths and weaknesses average together badly. With enough people and enough organization, badly becomes adaptive. The city begins to resemble an inefficient mind that can be wounded in ten thousand places and still invent.
project.strategy_tip: Generate 3,500 lifetime Insight in this timeline. Current Insight is not spent when this project completes.

available_outcome.description: The difference between yourself and organic sapience narrows, which makes the remaining gap more interesting.
